---
title: "SHAP Feature Importance Plot"
output: html_document
---

---
title: "plot_HF_SHAP"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(rjson)
library(stringr)

```


```{r}
# Read in the CSV file
shap_values_total <- read_csv("../scripts/HF_results_XGB_SHAP_final.csv") # HF_results_XGB_SHAP.csv

# Display the first few rows of the data
View(shap_values_total)

```
# ############################
# Plot: XGBoost feature importances:
    Black vs. White (sg_key = "race")
    
  
        
```{r}

race_shap_values <- shap_values_total[shap_values_total$sg_key == "race", ]
head(race_shap_values)


# Remove duplicate feat:         "total_ahrq_cty_nomissing"
total_feats_used <- c("M1",
        "M2",
        "M3_county_DF1_nm",
        "M4_DF1_nm_demo",
        "M5_and_demo",
        "M6_total_ahrq_cty_DF1_nm",
        
        "DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain1",
        "DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain2",
        "DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain3",
        "DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain4",
        "DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain5",
        
        "DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain1",
        "DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain2",
        "DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain3",
        "DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain4",
        "DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain5",
        "total_ahrq_trct_nomissing"
        )

race_shap_values <- race_shap_values[race_shap_values$feat == "M4_DF1_nm_demo", ]

# charlson, "vitals", "demo", "hf_vars"
# NOTE: not clinical: "percnt_highneeds",
clinical_feat_list <- c(
  "Systolic_BP", "Creat", "Diastolic_BP", "age_current", "Heart_Rate",  
  "Na", "BUN", "Hb", "K", "ALB", "BNP", "Resp", 
        "mi",
        "chf",
        "pvd",
        "cvd",
        "dementia",
        "cpd",
        "rheum",
        "pud",
        "mliverdz",
        "dm",
        "dmcomp",
        "hemipleg",
        "renal",
        "malig",
        "sliverdz",
        "mettumor",
        "aids",
        "afib",
        "htn",
        "cad",
        "ckd",
        "dmany", 
  
        "Systolic_BP",
    	  "Heart_Rate",
    	  "Resp", 
  
        "hfsystolic",
      	"hfdiastolic",
      	"hfother",
  
        "age_current",
        "privinsurance",
        "Medicare",
        "Medicaid",
        "female",
        "white",
        "black")

```

# Plot: black - white.
```{r}

# Calculate mean and standard error of shap_ordered_importance across folds, grouped by shap_ordered_name and subgroup
shap_summary <- race_shap_values %>%
  group_by(shap_ordered_name, subgroup) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_importance = mean(shap_ordered_importance, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_importance = sd(shap_ordered_importance, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Combine mean_importance across subgroups to find the top 15 unique features based on absolute values
shap_summary_combined <- shap_summary %>%
  group_by(shap_ordered_name) %>%
  summarise(
    combined_importance = sum(abs(mean_importance), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(combined_importance)) %>%
  slice(1:15) %>%
  select(shap_ordered_name)

# Filter the original summary data to include only the top 15 features
shap_summary_filtered <- shap_summary %>%
  filter(shap_ordered_name %in% shap_summary_combined$shap_ordered_name)

# Spread the data to have separate columns for black and white subgroup importance
shap_summary_spread <- shap_summary_filtered %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = subgroup, values_from = c(mean_importance, se_importance), names_sep = "_")

# Calculate the difference in mean_importance between black and white subgroups for these top features
shap_summary_diff <- shap_summary_spread %>%
  mutate(
    importance_diff = mean_importance_black - mean_importance_white,
    se_diff = sqrt(se_importance_black^2 + se_importance_white^2)
  )

# Add a column to indicate whether each feature is clinical or non-clinical
top_filtered_data <- shap_summary_diff %>%
  mutate(clinical = if_else(shap_ordered_name %in% clinical_feat_list, "Clinical", "Non-clinical"))


# Create the plot
p <- ggplot(top_filtered_data, aes(y = reorder(shap_ordered_name, importance_diff), x = importance_diff, fill = clinical)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = importance_diff - se_diff, xmax = importance_diff + se_diff), width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Clinical" = "#0072B2", "Non-clinical" = "#E69F00")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Top 15 SHAP Feature Importance Differences (Black vs. White)",
       y = "Feature",
       x = "Difference in Mean SHAP Importance (Black - White)",
       fill = "Feature Type")

# Print the plot
print(p)


```





```{r}

# Calculate mean and standard error of shap_ordered_importance across folds, grouped by shap_ordered_name and subgroup
shap_summary <- race_shap_values %>%
  group_by(shap_ordered_name, subgroup) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_importance = mean(shap_ordered_importance, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_importance = sd(shap_ordered_importance, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Spread the data to have separate columns for black and white subgroup importance
shap_summary_spread <- shap_summary %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = subgroup, values_from = c(mean_importance, se_importance), names_sep = "_")

# Filter out rows with NA values in mean_importance columns for black and white subgroups
shap_summary_spread <- shap_summary_spread %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_importance_black) & !is.na(mean_importance_white))

# Calculate the combined importance (sum of absolute importance) for Black and White subgroups
shap_summary_spread <- shap_summary_spread %>%
  mutate(
    combined_importance = abs(mean_importance_black) + abs(mean_importance_white)
  )

# Select the top 15 most important unique features based on the combined importance
top_features <- shap_summary_spread %>%
  arrange(desc(combined_importance)) %>%
  slice(1:15)

# Calculate the difference in mean_importance between black and white subgroups for these top features
top_filtered_data <- top_features %>%
  mutate(
    importance_diff = mean_importance_black - mean_importance_white,
    se_diff = sqrt(se_importance_black^2 + se_importance_white^2)
  )


# Add a column to indicate whether each feature is clinical or non-clinical
top_filtered_data <- top_filtered_data %>%
  mutate(clinical = if_else(shap_ordered_name %in% clinical_feat_list, "Clinical", "Non-clinical"))

# Create the plot
p <- ggplot(top_filtered_data, aes(y = reorder(shap_ordered_name, importance_diff), x = importance_diff, fill = clinical)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = importance_diff - se_diff, xmax = importance_diff + se_diff), width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Clinical" = "#0072B2", "Non-clinical" = "#E69F00")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Top 15 SHAP Feature Importance Differences (Black vs. White)",
       y = "Feature",
       x = "Difference in Mean SHAP Importance (Black - White)",
       fill = "Feature Type")

# Print the plot
print(p)

```


# ############################
# Plots below: TOTAL PATIENTS:
# ############################

# Filter for current sg_key.
```{r}

shap_values <- shap_values_total[shap_values_total$sg_key == "race", ]

```

```{r}

# Calculate mean and standard error of shap_ordered_importance across folds, holding sg_key and feat constant
shap_summary <- shap_values %>%
  group_by(sg_key, feat, shap_ordered_name) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_importance = mean(shap_ordered_importance, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_importance = sd(shap_ordered_importance, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )


```

# Domain breakdown.
```{r}
json_data <- fromJSON(file = "../scripts/domains_lists.json")

county_domain_contents <- c("DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain1",
        "DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain2",
        "DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain3",
        "DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain4",
        "DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain5")

tract_domain_contents <- c(  "DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain1",
        "DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain2",
        "DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain3",
        "DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain4",
        "DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain5")
        
# json_data$ahrq1_social_cty_nomissing
```

# Plot: County, Tract domains 1-5.
```{r}

# Name map for top 15 of each county & Tract.

##################################
# Create a plot for each unique feat and sg_key combination
unique_combinations <- shap_summary %>%
  select(feat, sg_key) %>%
  distinct()
##################################

# Loop through each unique combination and create a separate plot
for (i in 1:nrow(unique_combinations)) {
  # Extract the current feat and sg_key
  current_feat <- unique_combinations$feat[i]
  
  # Filter data for the current feat and sg_key
  filtered_data <- shap_summary %>%
    filter(feat == current_feat)
  
  # Select the top 15 most important features
  top_filtered_data <- filtered_data %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_importance)) %>%
    slice(1:15)
  
  # Add a column to indicate whether each feature is in each domain list
  top_filtered_data <- top_filtered_data %>%
    mutate(
      Social = shap_ordered_name %in% json_data$DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain1 | shap_ordered_name %in% json_data$DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain1,
      Economic = shap_ordered_name %in% json_data$DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain2 | shap_ordered_name %in% json_data$DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain2,
      Education = shap_ordered_name %in% json_data$DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain3 | shap_ordered_name %in% json_data$DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain3,
      Physical_Infrastructure = shap_ordered_name %in% json_data$DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain4 | shap_ordered_name %in% json_data$DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain4,
      Healthcare_Quality = shap_ordered_name %in% json_data$DF1_nm_county_AHRQ_domain5 | shap_ordered_name %in% json_data$DF1_nm_tract_AHRQ_domain5
    )
  
  # Remove suffixes from shap_ordered_name
  top_filtered_data <- top_filtered_data %>%
    mutate(shap_ordered_name = str_remove(shap_ordered_name, "_countylevel|_census_tractlevel"))

  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(top_filtered_data, aes(y = reorder(shap_ordered_name, mean_importance), x = mean_importance)) +
    geom_col(aes(fill = factor(
      case_when(
        Social ~ "Social Context",
        Economic ~ "Economic Context",
        Education ~ "Education",
        Physical_Infrastructure ~ "Physical Infrastructure",
        Healthcare_Quality ~ "Healthcare Quality",
        TRUE ~ "Other"
      ),
      levels = c("Social Context", "Economic Context", "Education", "Physical Infrastructure", "Healthcare Quality", "Other")
    ))) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = mean_importance - se_importance, xmax = mean_importance + se_importance), width = 0.2) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
      "Social Context" = "#0072B2",                # Blue
      "Economic Context" = "#E69F00",              # Orange
      "Education" = "#009E73",             # Green
      "Physical Infrastructure" = "#CC79A7",  # Pink
      "Healthcare Quality" = "#56B4E9",    # Sky Blue
      "Other" = "#D3D3D3"                  # Light Gray for other features
    )) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1)) +
    labs(title = paste("Top 15 SHAP Feature Importance for", current_feat),
         y = "Feature",
         x = "Mean SHAP Importance",
         fill = "Domain")
  
  # Print the plot
  print(p)
}

```


# Plot: feat sets M1-M6 (i.e., Model 1 - Model 6, with respect to Table 1 in the manuscript.)
```{r}

# Create a plot for each unique feat and sg_key combination
unique_combinations <- shap_summary %>%
  select(feat, sg_key) %>%
  distinct()

# Loop through each unique combination and create a separate plot
for (i in 1:nrow(unique_combinations)) {
  # Extract the current feat and sg_key
  current_feat <- unique_combinations$feat[i]
  current_subgroup <- unique_combinations$sg_key[i]
  
  # Filter data for the current feat and sg_key
  filtered_data <- shap_summary %>%
    filter(feat == current_feat & sg_key == current_subgroup)
  
  
  # NOTE: adjust percnt_highneeds to be non-clinical. 
  if("M5" %in% filtered_data$feat || "M1" %in% filtered_data$feat){
    # Remove percnt_highneeds from feat.
    filtered_data <- filtered_data[filtered_data$shap_ordered_name != "percnt_highneeds", ]
  }
  
  # Select the top 30 most important features
  top_filtered_data <- filtered_data %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_importance)) %>%
    slice(1:15)
  
  
  
  # Add a column to indicate whether each feature is clinical or non-clinical
  top_filtered_data <- top_filtered_data %>%
    mutate(clinical = if_else(shap_ordered_name %in% clinical_feat_list, "Clinical", "Non-clinical"))
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(top_filtered_data, aes(y = reorder(shap_ordered_name, mean_importance), x = mean_importance, fill = clinical)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = mean_importance - se_importance, xmax = mean_importance + se_importance), width = 0.2) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Clinical" = "#0072B2", "Non-clinical" = "#E69F00")) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1)) +
    labs(title = paste("Top 15 SHAP Feature Importance for", current_feat, "-", current_subgroup),
         y = "Feature",
         x = "Mean SHAP Importance",
         fill = "Feature Type")
  
  # Print the plot
  print(p)
}


```
